records.ety: [OE. speld, AS. speld a spill to light a candle with; akin to D. speld a pin, OD. spelle, G. spalten to split, OHG. spaltan, MHG. spelte a splinter, Icel. spjald a square tablet, Goth. spilda a writing tablet. Cf. Spillsplinter, roll of paper, Spell to tell the letters of.]

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Spelled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Spelling.]

defs: To supply the place of for a time; to take the turn of, at work; to relieve; as, to spell the helmsman.

The relief of one person by another in any piece of work or watching; also, a turn at work which is carried on by one person or gang relieving another; as, a spell at the pumps; a spell at the masthead.
quotes: A spell at the wheel is called a trick.

defs: The time during which one person or gang works until relieved; hence, any relatively short period of time, whether a few hours, days, or weeks.
quotes: Nothing new has happened in this quarter, except the setting in of a severe spell of cold weather.

defs: One of two or more persons or gangs who work by spells.
quotes: Their toil is so extreme that they can not endure it above four hours in a day, but are succeeded by spells.

defs: A gratuitous helping forward of another's work; as, a logging spell.

records.ety: [AS. spell a saying, tale, speech; akin to OS. & OHG. spel, Icel. spjall,Goth. spill. Cf. Gospel, Spell to tell the letters of.]

defs: A stanza, verse, or phrase supposed to be endowed with magical power; an incantation; hence, any charm.
quotes: Start not; her actions shall be holy as You hear my spell is lawful.

records.ety: [OE. spellen, spellien, tell, relate, AS. spellian, fr. spell a saying, tale; akin to MHG. spellen to relate, Goth. spill<?/n.e Spell a tale. In sense 4 and those following, OE. spellen, perhaps originally a different word, and from or influenced by spell a splinter, from the use of a piece of wood to point to the letters in schools: cf. D. spellen to spell. Cf. Spell splinter.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Spelled (<?/) or Spelt (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Spelling.]

defs: To tell; to relate; to teach.
quotes: Might I that legend find, By fairies spelt in mystic rhymes.

defs: To put under the influence of a spell; to affect by a spell; to bewitch; to fascinate; to charm.
quotes: He was much spelled with Eleanor Talbot.
The Saxon heptarchy, when seven kings put together did spell but one in effect.

defs: To tell or name in their proper order letters of, as a word; to write or print in order the letters of, esp. the proper letters; to form, as words, by correct orthography.
quotes: The word “satire” ought to be spelled with i, and not with y.

defs: To discover by characters or marks; to read with difficulty; -- usually with out; as, to spell out the sense of an author; to spell out a verse in the Bible.
quotes: To spell out a God in the works of creation.
To sit spelling and observing divine justice upon every accident.

defs: To form words with letters, esp. with the proper letters, either orally or in writing.
quotes: When what small knowledge was, in them did dwell, And he a god, who could but read or spell.

defs: To study by noting characters; to gain knowledge or learn the meaning of anything, by study.
quotes: Where I may sit and rightly spell Of every star that heaven doth shew, And every herb that sips the dew.
